description:
# Why

SKILL.md's "Working in a multi-repo hub" section (corrected by bl-4f29,
landed 88f1206) describes `task.repo` as it behaved *before* bl-8994:

- "that field is create-anchored ... (bl-8994 tracks tightening this)"
- "`repo` is auto-set at create from the creating clone's `origin` URL
  (else its repo path)"

Both clauses are now stale. bl-8994 (landed dda0ad4) shipped: `repo` is
re-anchored at `bl claim` to the claiming clone — the definitive code
home — and only a fetchable `origin` URL is ever auto-written. The
`(else its repo path)` basename fallback was removed for `repo`; a
clone with no origin now leaves it null.

bl-4f29 explicitly deferred the provenance-correctness story to bl-8994
("bl-8994 tracks tightening this"), so this was a foreseeable sequencing
gap, not a new bug — but the text now actively misinforms an agent
reading it: it will expect `repo` to point at where a ball was filed
and to always be populated.

# Scope

In SKILL.md's multi-repo section, correct the `repo`-field description:

- `repo` is anchored at `bl claim`, not create. `bl create` stamps the
  creating clone's origin as a provisional value; `bl claim` re-anchors
  it to the claiming clone, which is definitionally the code home.
- Only a fetchable `origin` URL is auto-written — never a basename or
  path. A clone with no origin leaves `repo` null; null means "origin
  unknown," not "single-repo."
- Drop the "(bl-8994 tracks tightening this)" parenthetical — bl-8994
  is done.
- `bl update <id> repo=<url>` is the explicit fixup path; the field is
  implicitly frozen after claim but not locked.

The surrounding guidance (filter on `repo`, tag as a manual override,
parent + per-repo children) stays correct — this is a precision fix to
the one stale paragraph, not a rewrite.

# Origin

Noticed closing bl-8994: bl-4f29's SKILL.md correction landed an hour
before bl-8994 and described the pre-bl-8994 behavior.
title: SKILL.md: repo field is claim-anchored, not create-anchored
